oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Attracting and retaining new veterinarians
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Martens, Alan
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: mentoring
retention
recruitment
internship
externship
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Long gone are the days when you can throw a newly graduat­ing veterinarian into an ambulatory vehicle and expect them to succeed. Veterinary colleges are expected to teach veterinar­ians in four years what human medical colleges teach in four years plus a two-to-four-year residency and additionally teach about multiple species, and at a slower pace. Practice owners need to invest time and energy into new graduates. They need to mentor for months and years. From our practice perspective, the financial investment in new graduates is enormous. This financial return has to be balanced with long term practice stability, client satisfaction, and re­duced work-load for existing veterinarians in the practice.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 145-146
